---
title: "modelling clutch size"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(here)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
library(lme4)
library(performance)
library(MASS)
```

```{r}

results <- read.csv(here("data", "clean data", "results.csv"), colClasses = c(sex = "character"))

#make maternal ID a factor
results$maternalID <- as.factor(results$maternalID)

#make a treatment combination column
results <- results %>%
  mutate(treatment_combination = paste(larval_treatment, pupal_treatment, sep = "_"))  %>%
  mutate()


#remove ambient, because these need to be modelled separately. also remove larval treatment of cool and mean, because the sample size isn't big enough to model - just not enough clutches.
#I think I will model the ambient larva treatment separately.
results_filtered <- results %>%
  filter(pupal_treatment != "ambient",
         larval_treatment != "ambient",
         larval_treatment != "cool",
         larval_treatment != "mean",
         larval_treatment != "wild") %>%
  mutate(numerical_pupal_treatment = case_when(
    pupal_treatment == "cool"  ~ -1.5,
    pupal_treatment == "mean"  ~  0.0,
    pupal_treatment == "warm"  ~  1.5,
    pupal_treatment == "hot"   ~  2.5,
    TRUE ~ NA_real_
  )) %>%
  mutate(numerical_larval_treatment = case_when(
    larval_treatment == "warm"  ~  1.5,
    larval_treatment == "hot"   ~  2.5,
    TRUE ~ NA_real_
  )) %>%
  mutate(weight_mg = weight * 1000) %>%
  mutate(weight_mg_centered = weight_mg - mean(weight_mg, na.rm = TRUE))


results_filtered <- results_filtered %>%
  mutate(
    larval_treatment = factor(larval_treatment, levels = c("warm","hot")),
    pupal_treatment = factor(pupal_treatment, levels = c("cool","mean","warm","hot"))
  )

results_filtered <- results_filtered %>%
  mutate(
  pupal_treatment_centered  = numerical_pupal_treatment  + 1.5,
   larval_treatment_centered = numerical_larval_treatment - 1.5)

#how many eggs were counted in total
sum(results$clutch_size)
```

```{r}
#look at the distribution of clutch size
ggplot(results, aes(x = clutch_size)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 10) +  # change this value to control bin width +
  labs(title = "Distribution of clutch size",
       x = "clutch size",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()


# mean
mean(results_filtered$clutch_size, na.rm = TRUE)

# variance
var(results_filtered$clutch_size, na.rm = TRUE)

#look at the distribution of female mass
ggplot(results, aes(x = weight)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.005) +  # change this value to control bin width +
  labs(title = "Distribution of female mass",
       x = "female mass",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()

#look at the distribution of mean egg sizes
ggplot(results, aes(x = egg_mean_1A)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.01) +  # change this value to control bin width +
  labs(title = "Distribution of mean egg size",
       x = "mean egg size",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}

#full model

lm1 <-  glmer(clutch_size ~ numerical_pupal_treatment + numerical_larval_treatment + weight_mg_centered + (1|maternalID),  family = poisson(link = "log"), data = results_filtered)

summary(lm1)


#using an identity link means that no data transformation is applied
#I have centered female weight so that centered weight = 0 corresponds to the mean female weight


```


```{r}



lm1 <-  glm(clutch_size ~ numerical_larval_treatment, family = poisson(link = "identity"), data =  results_filtered)
summary(lm1)



lm2 <-  glm(clutch_size ~ numerical_larval_treatment + weight_mg_centered, family = poisson(link = "identity"), data =  results_filtered)
summary(lm2)


lm3 <-  glm(clutch_size ~ weight_mg_centered, family = poisson(link = "identity"), data =  results_filtered)
summary(lm3)

lm4 <-  glm(clutch_size ~ numerical_larval_treatment + numerical_pupal_treatment + weight_mg_centered, family = poisson(link = "identity"), data =  results_filtered)
summary(lm4)



lm5 <-  glmer(clutch_size ~ numerical_larval_treatment + numerical_pupal_treatment + weight_mg_centered + (1|maternalID), family = poisson(link = "identity"), data =  results_filtered)
summary(lm5)


lm6 <-  glmer(clutch_size ~ numerical_larval_treatment + numerical_pupal_treatment + weight_mg_centered + (1|maternalID), family = poisson(link = "log"), data =  results_filtered)
summary(lm6)

lm7 <-  glmer(clutch_size ~ numerical_larval_treatment * numerical_pupal_treatment + weight_mg_centered + (1|maternalID), family = poisson(link = "log"), data =  results_filtered)
summary(lm7)
plot(lm7)

lm7_nb <- glm.nb( clutch_size ~ numerical_larval_treatment + numerical_pupal_treatment + weight_mg_centered, data = results_filtered)

summary(lm7_nb)


lm8 <-  lmer(clutch_size ~ numerical_larval_treatment * numerical_pupal_treatment + weight_mg_centered + (1|maternalID), data =  results_filtered)
summary(lm8)

plot(lm8)

```


THOSE THAT HAD AMBIENT LARVAL TREATMENT
```{r}
#going to try modelling those that had ambient larval temp

results <- read.csv(here("data", "clean data", "results.csv"), colClasses = c(sex = "character"))

#make maternal ID a factor
results$maternalID <- as.factor(results$maternalID)

#make a treatment combination column
results <- results %>%
  mutate(treatment_combination = paste(larval_treatment, pupal_treatment, sep = "_"))  %>%
  mutate()


#remove ambient, because these need to be modelled separately. also remove larval treatment of cool and mean, because the sample size isn't big enough to model - just not enough clutches.
#I think I will model the ambient larva treatment separately.
results_ambient_larval <- results %>%
  mutate(numerical_pupal_treatment = case_when(
    pupal_treatment == "cool"  ~ -1.5,
    pupal_treatment == "mean"  ~  0.0,
    pupal_treatment == "warm"  ~  1.5,
    pupal_treatment == "hot"   ~  2.5,
    TRUE ~ NA_real_ )) %>%
    filter(
         larval_treatment == "ambient",
         pupal_treatment != "ambient") %>%
  mutate(weight_mg = weight * 1000) %>%
  mutate(weight_mg_centered = weight_mg - mean(weight_mg, na.rm = TRUE))


results_ambient_larval <- results_filtered %>%
  mutate(
    pupal_treatment = factor(pupal_treatment, levels = c("cool","mean","warm","hot"))
  )

```



```{r}
lm1_nb <- glmer.nb( clutch_size ~ numerical_pupal_treatment + weight_mg_centered + (1|maternalID), data = results_filtered)

summary(lm1_nb)
```
